name: ETH/SOL Depth Threshold Portfolio Impact Diagnostic
edge_rationale: ETH and SOL asset-specific optimization both favored min_sweep_depth_pct 0.0075, but the tighter threshold reduces trade frequency. Before changing SOL shadow behavior or moving toward BTC/ETH/SOL PAPER, the portfolio impact of 0.00649 versus 0.0075 must be measured directly.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: Higher sweep-depth thresholds may filter lower-quality stop-runs, improving ER/PF and drawdown, but may also remove useful independent signals that improve multi-asset portfolio frequency and diversification.
required_data: Frozen trial-00095 params from research store
baseline_reference: Current production shadow profile uses BTC 0.00649, ETH 0.0075, and SOL 0.00649. ETH/SOL asset-specific optimization reports selected 0.0075 with fewer trades and higher ER/PF.
frozen_assumptions: No production, PAPER, LIVE, runtime, core, execution, orchestrator, settings, sidecar, M4, or production DB changes.
Only ETH/SOL min_sweep_depth_pct scenario inputs vary.
All non-depth trial-00095 parameters remain frozen.
BTC/ETH candidate risk remains 0.35%; SOL candidate risk remains 0.15%.
This diagnostic cannot approve PAPER or LIVE.
expected_observation: If ETH/SOL asset-specific depth is portfolio-beneficial, the ETH/SOL 0.0075 scenario should retain enough trade frequency while maintaining low correlation, low overlap, and acceptable portfolio drawdown versus the current shadow profile.

kill_criteria.source_db_mutation: Mutating audited source snapshots invalidates this milestone.
kill_criteria.parameter_expansion: Changing parameters beyond ETH/SOL min_sweep_depth_pct invalidates this milestone.
kill_criteria.promotion_claim: Claiming PAPER/LIVE/runtime approval invalidates this milestone.
kill_criteria.missing_data_substitution: Substituting unaudited data if a source snapshot is unavailable invalidates this milestone.

failure_modes: Asset-specific thresholds improve standalone ER/PF but reduce frequency too much.
Lower SOL threshold adds useful diversification despite worse standalone drawdown.
Portfolio gate vetoes obscure standalone trade-count changes.
OOS sample is too short to decide threshold promotion without forward shadow evidence.
Same-bar overlap or daily correlation rises under one threshold profile.
author: Codex
